segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What was the difference between Bruce's behavior and that of other people?

translations.zh-Hans: 布鲁斯的行为与其他人有何不同？

segments.text: The rough road across the plain soon became so bad that we tried to get Bruce to drive back to the village we had come from.

translations.zh-Hans: 横穿平原的崎岖道路很快变得糟糕透顶，我们试图劝布鲁斯开回出发的村庄。

segments.text: Even though the road was littered with boulders and pitted with holes, Bruce was not in the least perturbed.

translations.zh-Hans: 尽管路上遍布巨石、坑洼不平，布鲁斯却一点也不担心。

segments.text: Glancing at his map, he informed us that the next village was a mere 20 miles away.

translations.zh-Hans: 他瞥了一眼地图，告诉我们下一个村庄只有20英里远。

segments.text: It was not that Bruce always underestimated difficulties.

translations.zh-Hans: 这倒不是说布鲁斯总是低估困难。

segments.text: He simply had no sense of danger at all.

translations.zh-Hans: 他根本就没有危险意识。

segments.text: No matter what the conditions were, he believed that a car should be driven as fast as it could possibly go.

translations.zh-Hans: 无论路况如何，他都认为汽车应该开到所能达到的最快速度。

segments.text: As we bumped over the dusty track, we swerved to avoid large boulders.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们在尘土飞扬的小路上颠簸前行，不时转向避开巨石。

segments.text: The wheels scooped up stones which hammered ominously under the car.

translations.zh-Hans: 车轮卷起的石块不祥地敲击着车底。

segments.text: We felt sure that sooner or later, a stone would rip a hole in our petrol tank or damage the engine.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们确信，迟早会有石头刺破油箱或损坏发动机。

segments.text: Because of this, we kept looking back, wondering if we were leaving a trail of oil and petrol behind us.

translations.zh-Hans: 因此我们不断回头看，担心身后是否留下了一路机油和汽油。

segments.text: What a relief it was when the boulders suddenly disappeared, giving way to a stretch of plain where the only obstacles were clumps of bushes.

translations.zh-Hans: 巨石突然消失，前面换成一片平原，唯一的障碍只是丛丛灌木，我们真是如释重负。

segments.text: But there was worse to come.

translations.zh-Hans: 但更糟的还在后面。

segments.text: Just ahead of us, there was a huge fissure.

translations.zh-Hans: 就在前方有一道巨大的裂缝。

segments.text: In response to renewed pleadings, Bruce stopped.

translations.zh-Hans: 经我们再次恳求，布鲁斯停了车。

segments.text: Though we all got out to examine the fissure, he remained in the car.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们都下车查看裂缝，他却仍坐在车里。

segments.text: We informed him that the fissure extended for 50 yards and was two feet wide and four feet deep.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们告诉他，裂缝长50码、宽2英尺、深4英尺。

segments.text: Even this had no effect.

translations.zh-Hans: 即使如此，他也无动于衷。

segments.text: Bruce went into a low gear and drove at a terrifying speed, keeping the front wheels astride the crack as he followed its zigzag course.

translations.zh-Hans: 布鲁斯挂上低速挡，以令人胆战心惊的速度行驶，让两个前轮跨在裂缝两侧，沿着它曲折的走向前进。

segments.text: Before we had time to worry about what might happen, we were back on the plane again.

translations.zh-Hans: 还没等我们担心会出什么事，我们就又回到了平地上。

segments.text: Bruce consulted the map once more and told us that the village was now only 15 miles away.

translations.zh-Hans: 布鲁斯再次查看地图，告诉我们村庄现在只有15英里远。

segments.text: Our next obstacle was a shallow pool of water about half a mile across.

translations.zh-Hans: 下一个障碍是一片宽约半英里的浅水。

segments.text: Bruce charged at it, but in the middle, the car came to a grinding halt.

translations.zh-Hans: 布鲁斯猛冲过去，但开到中央时，汽车嘎然而止。

segments.text: A yellow light on the dashboard flashed angrily, and Bruce cheerfully announced that there was no oil in the engine.

translations.zh-Hans: 仪表盘上一盏黄灯气势汹汹地闪着，布鲁斯却兴高采烈地宣布发动机里没油了。
